name: Aljibe Pools Circular Trail
name_original: Cascada del Aljibe y Puente de los Trillos — trailrunning circular desde Roblelacasa
description: Jog the difficult 6.9-mile Aljibe Pools Circular Trail in Guadalajara, featuring the spectacular Aljibe Waterfalls and charming villages.
region_intro: Jogging around Campillo De Ranas offers a chance to explore a landscape characterized by rugged hills, dense woodlands, and river valleys. The region features varied terrain, including significant elevation changes, making it suitable for runners seeking a challenge. Many running routes traverse natural parks and protected areas, providing scenic views and a connection to nature. Best jogging routes around Campillo De Ranas The most popular jogging route is Arcipreste de Hita Trail, a 9.5 miles (15.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours to complete, featuring challenging ascents through natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Aljibe Pools Circular Trail, a difficult 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path that winds through hilly terrain and offers views of natural pools. Local runners also love the Campillo de Ranas – Pico Ocejón loop from Majaelrayo, a 12.1 miles (19.5 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours. Jogging around Campillo De Ranas is defined by challenging hill climbs, scenic river valleys, and extensive woodlands. The network of running routes primarily offers options for experienced runners seeking demanding trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Campillo De Ranas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Campillo De Ranas?
A: The region is characterized by rugged hills, dense woodlands, and river valleys. You'll encounter varied terrain with significant elevation changes, making it suitable for runners seeking a challenge. Many routes traverse natural parks and protected areas, offering scenic views and a deep connection to nature.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running trails in Campillo De Ranas?
A: Campillo De Ranas primarily offers routes for experienced runners. Out of 37 available routes, 33 are rated as difficult, and 4 are moderate. There are no easy routes listed, so beginners should be prepared for challenging terrain and significant elevation gains.

Q: What are the typical lengths of running trails in this area?
A: Running trails around Campillo De Ranas vary in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031390028/arcipreste-de-hita-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcipreste de Hita Trail&lt;/a&gt; is about 9.5 miles (15.4 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campillo de Ranas – Pico Ocejón loop from Majaelrayo&lt;/a&gt; extends to 12.1 miles (19.5 km). Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Ocejón – Pico Ocejón loop from Majaelrayo&lt;/a&gt;, are around 8.7 miles (14.1 km).

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Campillo De Ranas?
A: Yes, several running routes in Campillo De Ranas are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030895550/aljibe-pools-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aljibe Pools Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 6.9-mile (11.1 km) path winding through hilly terrain. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Ocejón – Pico Ocejón loop from Majaelrayo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or natural attractions can I find along the running trails?
A: The region offers numerous natural attractions. You can find beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3820812/lakes-around-campillo-de-ranas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;lakes&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3820811/waterfalls-around-campillo-de-ranas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. Notable highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2699850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the reservoir from the Royuelo ravine&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6552563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Pool at El Molino Caído&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4157330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despeñalagua Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Campillo De Ranas suitable for family-friendly running or jogging?
A: Given the predominantly difficult terrain and significant elevation changes, Campillo De Ranas is generally not ideal for casual family jogging with young children. The routes are more suited for experienced runners looking for a challenging workout in natural landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Campillo De Ranas?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for running in Campillo De Ranas, offering milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, especially at midday, while winters in this mountainous region may bring cold weather, snow, and ice, requiring appropriate gear and caution.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running routes in Campillo De Ranas?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many natural areas in Spain permit dogs on trails, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage or park regulations for any specific restrictions in protected areas you plan to visit with your dog.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails?
A: Parking is typically available in or near the villages that serve as starting points for many routes, such as Campillo de Ranas or Majaelrayo. Look for designated parking areas or roadside spots, especially near trailheads. Arriving early, particularly on weekends, is recommended to secure a spot.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by cafes or places to refuel?
A: The running routes in Campillo De Ranas primarily traverse natural and often remote landscapes. While some villages may have small cafes or bars, routes are not specifically designed to pass by frequent refreshment stops. It's best to carry sufficient water and snacks, and plan to refuel in a village before or after your run.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Campillo De Ranas?
A: One of the longest running routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Ocejón – Campillejo loop from Campillo de Ranas&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 16.2 miles (26.1 km) and involves significant elevation gain, making it a demanding option for endurance runners.
tips: A district of the municipality of Campillo de Ranas, located to the northwest of the province of Guadalajara, El Espinar currently has barely ten and a half permanent inhabitants. This does not prevent its few buildings from offering visitors excellent samples of popular architecture, framed in what is called black architecture of the Sierra del Ocejón.

Although its access is steep and difficult, the Aljibe Waterfall is a popular place for hiking lovers. The path that leads to it offers spectacular views and is part of the Aljibe Waterfall Route. For long stretches, the trail runs close to the ravine without side protection, so caution should be taken.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Aljibe Pools Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail, designed for jogging, usually takes around 1 hour and 34 minutes to complete. This estimate is for the full 11 km circular route.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The Aljibe Pools Circular Trail is rated as difficult. While generally not overly complicated, it features sections with quite steep slopes, especially when descending rapidly towards the Soto stream near the waterfalls. Caution is advised in these areas to prevent slipping. The route is marked with white and yellow signs.

faqs.question: Is the Aljibe Pools Circular Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and sections with steep slopes, this trail might be challenging for beginner joggers. Those new to trail jogging should be prepared for varied terrain and elevation changes.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail links the villages of El Espinar and Roblelacasa. You can typically start from either village. Parking availability can vary, especially during peak times, so it's advisable to arrive early.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features and landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The highlight of the route is undoubtedly the Aljibe Waterfalls, also known as the Matallana Pools, which consist of two cascades. You'll also pass through charming villages like El Espinar and encounter typical slate fences of the region.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Aljibe Pools Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to experience the full beauty and spectacularity of the waterfalls, particularly their water flow, is during spring and autumn. The scenery is picturesque during these seasons.

faqs.question: Is the Aljibe Pools Circular Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially in areas with wildlife or other hikers, and be mindful of local regulations regarding leashes within the Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the trail or the Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Aljibe Pools Circular Trail itself or the Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara. However, it's always good practice to check for any updated local regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are viewpoints near the Aljibe Waterfalls that allow for an optimal appreciation of the cascades from the opposite side of the stream.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the 'Circular Pozas del Aljibe' and the 'Pueblos de la Arquitectura Negra (Etapa 4)' trails.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and steep sections, we recommend wearing appropriate trail running shoes with good grip. Layers of clothing are advisable, especially in spring and autumn, and always carry enough water, as there are no specific water points on the trail itself.
